seoTitle: How to Make a Calendar Dashboard in Power BI by Day
metaDescription: Learn how to build a calendar dashboard in Power BI that visualizes data day by day, ideal for tracking deadlines, sales, and daily trends.
intro: Some data just makes more sense laid out like a calendar, especially when you're tracking deadlines, daily sales, or attendance patterns. This guide walks through building a calendar-style dashboard in Power BI that visualizes your metrics day by day. It's aimed at anyone who needs a daily view rather than a standard line chart or table.

steps.heading: Collect the necessary data
steps.body: Make sure your dataset includes a proper date field along with the daily metric you want to display, such as sales totals or task counts per day.

steps.heading: Create the calendar visual
steps.body: Use a calendar-style visual, whether built-in or from a custom visual source, and connect your date and value fields to populate each day.

steps.heading: Customize your dashboard
steps.body: Adjust color scales, labels, and tooltips so each day's data is clear at a glance, and highlight important dates if relevant to your use case.

steps.heading: Add final touches and automation
steps.body: Once the layout looks right, connect the underlying data to an automatically refreshing source so the calendar stays current without manual updates.

faq.question: Does Power BI have a built-in calendar visual?
faq.answer: Power BI doesn't include a native calendar heatmap visual by default, so many users rely on a calendar-style custom visual from AppSource to achieve this layout.

faq.question: Can I use a calendar dashboard to track project deadlines?
faq.answer: Yes, mapping deadline dates to a calendar visual is a common and effective use case for keeping timelines visible at a glance.

faq.question: How do I highlight specific days, like weekends or holidays?
faq.answer: This typically depends on the specific calendar visual's formatting options, which often allow custom color rules based on date properties.

faq.question: Will the calendar dashboard update automatically each month?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as the date field in your data model is dynamic and the report refreshes on schedule, the calendar will reflect current and upcoming dates.
